diarized_transcript:
[Agent] Thank you for calling Genshin Impact Player Support, this is Taylor. How can I help?
[Caller] The server was down way longer than announced this morning. Are we getting compensation for that?
[Agent] Absolutely, and I appreciate you calling to check. Yes, compensation went out — let me pull your account so I can show you exactly what and when. UID please?
[Caller] 704582103.
[Agent] Thanks, Sofia. So here's what happened: scheduled maintenance was advertised as 5 hours but ran 7 hours 20 minutes due to a database migration that took longer than expected. Per our overrun policy, players receive 300 Primogems as apology compensation — 60 for the scheduled window plus 240 additional for the overrun.
[Caller] OK. Where do I find it?
[Agent] It was delivered via in-game mail. Looking at your mail log — yes, sent at 08:42 UTC this morning, subject 'Update Maintenance Notice.' It's valid for 30 days before it auto-expires, so you have plenty of time to claim it.
[Caller] Hmm, I don't remember seeing it. Let me check... oh, I see it now. I just hadn't scrolled down. 300 primogems.
[Agent] Perfect, there it is. Tap Claim and the primogems land in your wallet instantly.
[Caller] Got it, claimed. Is there any extra compensation because the overrun was long?
[Agent] The 300 already accounts for the overrun per our tiered policy — for context, 60 primogems is the flat maintenance apology, and each full hour of overrun adds 60 more. We rounded up on the partial hour in players' favor.
[Caller] OK, that's fair actually.
[Agent] I'll also send you a short email with our maintenance compensation policy for your records — handy to know the tiers. And if you ever don't see expected compensation, just call in and we'll verify it landed.
[Caller] Great. Thank you Taylor.
[Agent] You're very welcome, Sofia. Enjoy the game!
